title: Built a product roadmap system in Notion — features linked to user problems, effort/impact scoring, release tracking [9€]
body:
Been using Notion for product planning but kept struggling with two things: knowing *why* I was building something, and prioritizing without going on gut feel.

Built this template to solve it:

- Features database — status pipeline (Idea → Done), Effort (S/M/L/XL), Impact (Low–Critical), Priority (P0–P3), linked to releases and user problems
- User Problems database — the real problems behind your features: severity, how many users it affects, source (interview/survey/analytics), status
- Releases database — group features into versioned releases with target dates and release notes
- Feedback database — capture raw user feedback from email/Reddit/Twitter, link it to the feature it inspired

The workflow: add a User Problem before adding a Feature. If you can't name the problem, question whether to build the feature at all.

Works on Notion's free plan. EN.

→ [Gumroad link]

title: Notion Product Roadmap — Features, User Problems, Releases & Feedback
description: Plan what to build next in Notion. Link every feature to a real user problem. Score by Effort + Impact. Track releases. Capture feedback. One system, four databases. #notiontemplate #productmanagement #indiehacker #solofounder #roadmap

title: Build features users actually want — Notion template for indie makers
description: Stop building from gut feeling. This Notion template forces you to link every feature to a real user problem — and links user feedback all the way to the features it inspired. #productmanagement #notion #indiehackers #solofounder #productivity
